decode_mechanism: Browser image decode through HTMLImageElement (loadRasterImage, png.loadPngImage), drawn onto a 2D canvas
encode_mechanism: canvas.toBlob('image/png') (canvasToPngBlob, png.rgbaToPngBlob). png.encodeAnimatedPng also writes a single-frame PNG through UPNG (pure JS) when you pass one frame
notes: No WebAssembly and no ffmpeg on this path. png.isPngFile() classifies the file

decode_mechanism: UPNG.decode plus UPNG.toRGBA8 (pure JS, @pdf-lib/upng) through png.decodeAnimatedPng
encode_mechanism: UPNG.encode (pure JS) through png.encodeAnimatedPng; the loop count is patched into the acTL chunk by png.patchApngLoopCount
notes: The colorCount argument drives palette quantization, so this encoder is lossy by design. Frames go in and out as RGBA Uint8Array

decode_mechanism: WebCodecs ImageDecoder first, with a pure-JS JPEG decoder (from the inlined @cross/image) as the fallback: jpg.decodeJpegLikeSource asks for runtimeDecoding 'prefer'. Other paths simply decode through HTMLImageElement
encode_mechanism: MozJPEG WebAssembly (@jsquash/jpeg) through jpg.encodeJpegBlobFromImageData, which exposes progressive and chroma-subsampling flags. canvasToBlob(canvas, 'image/jpeg', quality) is the plain-canvas alternative
feature_detect: none exported (the MozJPEG path needs WebAssembly, the canvas path needs nothing)
notes: This is the only place the library ships a real codec of its own. jpg.inspectJpegStructure reads component count, progressive flag and Adobe transform straight from the bytes; EXIF is read with exifr

decode_mechanism: WebCodecs ImageDecoder with type image/heic, through jpg.decodeJpegLikeSource. The inlined @cross/image has no pure-JS HEIC fallback, so on a runtime whose ImageDecoder cannot handle image/heic the call throws
feature_detect: none exported for HEIC. jpg.isHeicFile() only classifies the file, it does not test the runtime
notes: Accepted extensions and types: .heic, .heif, image/heic, image/heif, image/heic-sequence. Decoded HEIC is re-encoded through the JPEG, PNG or WebP paths

decode_mechanism: Browser image decode through HTMLImageElement (webp.renderStillWebpImageData). webp.readWebpMetadataFromBytes parses the RIFF chunks in pure JS without decoding anything
encode_mechanism: OffscreenCanvas.convertToBlob({ type: 'image/webp' }) inside the inlined @cross/image (webp.recompressStillWebp, webp.optimizeWebp), with a pure-JS VP8L lossless encoder as the fallback when OffscreenCanvas is missing. canvasToBlob(canvas, 'image/webp', quality) is the plain-canvas alternative
notes: Two honest caveats in the encode path: the lossless flag only reaches the pure-JS VP8L fallback (the OffscreenCanvas branch passes quality only), and that branch does not check the returned blob type, unlike the AVIF branch

decode_mechanism: WebCodecs ImageDecoder, one frame at a time (webp.decodeAnimatedWebpFrames); each VideoFrame is drawn to a canvas and exported as PNG
encode_mechanism: ffmpeg.wasm with the libwebp_anim encoder, fed a PNG frame sequence and a concat manifest (webp.encodeAnimatedWebp)
feature_detect: webp.canDecodeAnimatedWebpInBrowser() to decode (checks ImageDecoder), gif.canUseFfmpegInBrowser() to encode
notes: Frame count and per-frame delays come from the pure-JS RIFF parse of the ANIM and ANMF chunks, not from the decoder

decode_mechanism: WebCodecs ImageDecoder with type image/avif, inside the inlined @cross/image (avif.readAvifSource). No WebAssembly AVIF decoder is shipped
encode_mechanism: OffscreenCanvas.convertToBlob({ type: 'image/avif', quality }) inside the inlined @cross/image. The browser's own AVIF encoder does the work; if the returned blob is not image/avif the call throws instead of handing back a PNG
feature_detect: avif.canProcessAvifInBrowser() (checks window.ImageDecoder and window.OffscreenCanvas). avif.assertAvifRuntime() throws the same check
notes: Still images only, there is no animated-AVIF path. Quality is 1 to 100; the presets balanced/smaller/aggressive map to 70/56/42

decode_mechanism: ffmpeg.wasm decodes the frames to PNG (gif.extractGifFrames, gif.extractGifPreviewFrame). gif.readGifMetadata reads size, frame count, delays, loop count and transparency from the bytes in pure JS. An HTMLImageElement decodes the first frame only
encode_mechanism: ffmpeg.wasm with a palettegen and paletteuse filter graph (gif.encodePngSequenceToGif, and the transforms optimizeGif, resizeGif, cropGif, trimGif, changeGifSpeed, reverseGif, splitGif)
notes: ffmpeg-core.js, ffmpeg-core.wasm and ffmpeg-core.worker.js are fetched from the jsDelivr CDN on first use and cached for the tab; the image itself is never part of that request

decode_mechanism: Not in this module. Decode a GIF with gif.extractGifFrames or an HTMLImageElement
encode_mechanism: Dependency-free pure-JS GIF89a writer (gifEncoder.encodeGif): median-cut quantization to at most 256 colours, then variable-width LZW. No ffmpeg, no WebAssembly, no network
notes: Writes fully opaque frames: GIF only carries 1-bit transparency, so flatten alpha onto a background first. One frame per call

encode_mechanism: Pure-JS ICONDIR and ICONDIRENTRY container packing canvas-encoded PNG payloads (ico.buildIcoBlob, ico.encodeIco, ico.renderPngAtSize, and png.createIcoBuffer)
notes: ico.ICO_SIZE_OPTIONS is 16, 32, 48, 64, 128, 256. ico.verifyIcoHeader re-reads the container before download. Sizes of 256 are written as 0 per the ICO spec

decode_mechanism: Browser image decode through HTMLImageElement: image/bmp is in rasterImageMimeTypes and isRasterImageFile() accepts .bmp
notes: There is no BMP module. BMP is only an accepted input to the shared canvas helpers, and it leaves as PNG, JPEG, WebP or AVIF

decode_mechanism: ffmpeg.wasm reads mp4, mov, m4v, webm, avi and mpeg input (gif.convertVideoToGif, webp.convertVideoToWebp, gif.extractVideoClipFramesAsPng)
encode_mechanism: ffmpeg.wasm from a PNG frame sequence (gif.convertGifToMp4, webp.convertWebpToMp4): yuv420p, crf 23, faststart, no audio track
notes: Video, listed because the GIF and animated-WebP paths convert to and from it. The source sets no -c:v, so ffmpeg's default encoder for the mp4 container is used

feature_detect: none: the library has no JPEG XL path
notes: Listed because it is the question people ask next. There is no JXL codec bundled, no canvas type and no WebCodecs type for it anywhere in src/
